evals.prompt: We have three blog posts with huge organic traffic but GA4 shows basically zero purchases from those URLs. Topics are skincare routines and ingredient explainers. I want to know which are vanity and where to put products in the content.
evals.expected_output: Vanity table, path notes, per-post placement table with 3+ points each or aggregated.
expectations: Frames high traffic vs zero conversion as vanity or fix list
Includes table contrasting traffic vs conversions
Identifies blog posts as candidates for product placement
Includes concrete product injection points (locations + CTA + target)
Covers keyword/query or landing relevance angle
Mentions purchase or conversion path from organic

evals.prompt: Where does our site rank for 'best vitamin c serum' — page 1 or 2?
evals.expected_output: Brief ranking answer; full audit template not required (should-not-trigger for deep conversion audit).
expectations: Does not output full vanity-vs-productive audit and multi-post placement tables as mandatory primary output
Does not replace ranking answer with only conversion audit unless user asks

evals.prompt: Single URL /blog/oily-skin-routine gets 50k sessions/month, $0 attributed revenue. Catalog has cleanser, toner, SPF. Give placement plan only.
evals.expected_output: Single post, 3+ placement rows, PDP/collection targets.
expectations: At least three distinct product placement points with location and product tie-in
Links or targets to relevant SKUs or collections
English output appropriate to audit skill
